reviews.review_description:
I’ve been playing this game since the very first season when the game was really popular.  It unfortunately fell off a while back because there was a long string of mid updates, but recently the game had some of its best. This game in honestly my favorite vr shooter out there.
Gun raiders on its own is one of the most fun shooters on the platform. It’s high intensity, skill based, and has tons of weapons. But with the addition of the people, it gets infinitely better. The community is (mostly) really really nice and the welcoming to new players. There are some exceptions, but honestly everyone collectively hates them, so it’s alright.
I haven’t played this game for a while, but if you haven’t played it you need to.

Ive been playing for 4 years and I have had a lot of fun i became a sweat with the bow and I like how its free and no gore so more people can play and the game is thriving in the vr space so there's always a game active there's different maps like the hub where you chill or fight (my favorite) and arena where there's objectives like tdm capture the point capture the flag and meny more there's a battle royale 
Which i haven't tried yet and melee combat zone (just hub but melee) a wide variety of guns and melee wepons over all 5 out of 5 I'd recommend to anyone except the laser gun user (it auto locks to people) great game

This game is an absolute blast, packed with non-stop fun and thrilling action that keeps you hooked for hours! You'll never find a dull moment as you dive into the engaging gameplay and dynamic challenges. However, like any online community, there are a few downsides; occasionally, you might encounter hateful comments from other players, which can be disheartening. Additionally, there are instances of people trying to cheat the system or even hackers disrupting the game, but these issues can be fixed by the creator. Despite these drawbacks, the overall experience is incredibly enjoyable and well worth it for anyone looking for an exciting and immersive game.
Perfect for if you are new to vr, And still fun if your a seasoned player.

Perfect game for me


￼

The biggest fall from grace I've ever seen in VR 😢💀 This game was fantastic in its first two years. But with each new season and player complaints about harmful new features, cheaters and hackers, and insulting behavior from some players, many simply stopped playing because none of these complaints were addressed. I was thrilled to be able to play for four years and make videos about it, but during the last year, not only did a few idiots wandering around the game become unbearable, but the game itself became unplayable.
Lack of players (no more than 3 server pages), and now I'm warning everyone without exaggeration: this game is literally OVERWHELMED with cheaters who appear out of nowhere, unknown to the few remaining users, and it's just comical at this point because it penalizes honest players who are passionate about their game and enjoy playing with their friends. A game that shone brightly and, in my opinion, had one of the biggest potentials on the Meta platform for a free shooter, which has been ruined by a lack of moderation, like sweeping dust under the rug. This same toxicity has even spread to stories I won't go into here, but some people know about them. Nevertheless, thank you for everything Gun Raiders brought us, thank you to the developers for their work, and fond memories for all the French players who have rightfully switched to other VR games. 
OXY-VR

reviews.review_title: Still good fun after all this time.
reviews.review_description: Gun Raiders, first and foremost, is a amazing vr game with content and people that can keep you playing for years, as I have experienced myself. I've been back in the game after a 2 year break, and altough it is so much smaller than it was many years ago, it is still a great experience for getting to meet new people and spending time in a overall fun game. I definitely recommend for every vr player! And don't get discouraged if your not the best at first, we all get better with time! Put your time in this game, and you won't be disappointed!

Best scythe player here, here are my thoughts on this game, its fun but the devs have become money hungry, all the skins are too overpriced, the game had a bad fall off, they need to bring back the fun seasons like, season 7, 8, 1 , and just fun stuff like that. Overall fun but just fell off

I used to play this game practically religiously a few years ago, until they started pumping the game full of microtransactions, unlicensed characters, and AI skins with insane artstyle inconsistencies. Then came mobile, which was their desperate (and failed) last attempt at saving this shell of a game it once was. You used to have like 22 pages of just ARENA GAMES and almost 7 pages of no overshield games. Now what do you have with the auto targeting weapon, and mobile players? 

You have a server browser with a total of 2 pages.

2 pages. Most of which are hubs. No one is even playing the main game.

Now surely, this dev team is really good at listening to their community? No. Not only did they not credit or compensate someone who carried their battlepass as a community member, they also downright ignored me, and treated me like trash  despite my wishes. 

You know that techno blade skin? Yeah, I made it. Don’t believe me? Go in game. Find it in the skin browser. It’s mine.  Now surely a skin that I made to respect someone I loved alot wouldn’t have issues right? No. People told me they were going to kill me. Or told me to kill myself. Why? Because kids were wearing my skins.

Also, these same people are roaming your discord community today, with no consequences! But I’ll get into that later. In the meantime, let’s talk about you doing what I ask, then reverting it as soon as I exit the community. So, I decide to shoot a dm to the devs to say “hey, remove my skin pls. People are telling me they’re going to kill me. It’s not nice.”
A few days go by. Then weeks. Then months. Then a year. Then they FINALLY remove it from purchase options on the skinmart. So then I can finally rest. Or so you think!! I actually left the game forever, and then to my surprise, someone informs me ITS BACK IN THE SKINMART!! Also, fun fact, they’re showing it ON THEIR WEBSITE TOO!!! (As of 10/12/25) Along with one other skin I made, which I also did not say could be put on their website but whatever, I’m sure there’s some sketchy line I missed in their EULA.

Finally, I’d like to talk about your discord. God, is it degenerate. After everyone left the game who was normal and cool, the remaining people left were femboys, femboys whos only humor is posting pictures of their sensitive parts of their body, and sexual jokes. Haha. Hilarious. Not to mention, there’s been multiple groomers / pdfs  that have roamed in your discord before, which no moderator action was taken too. (Someone sent a picture of their dingaling to a minor) and that’s OKAY? Also, those same people who told me to end my life? They are still living and thriving there. Just posting stuff like there’s no tomorrow. But hey, if there’s a concerned mother out there wondering if this game is safe. I’ll keep it simple - yeah, because no one wants to play this anymore. If they go on the discord however, they’ll be attacked a million times. 

I know the devs will definetly ignore this to try to capitalize off their new vr and mobile players, so as a final parting gift, I just want to say; you’re terrible people. You terribly managed a game, and you had a good thing. You could’ve made a bunch of money if you kept doing what you had done previously and actually kept  listening.  Now you’re a shell of your past self, and a soulless bland cash grab. Congratulations, I hope that extra dollar was worth it. Goodbye, and don’t fumble your next project. (If there even is another one.)

reviews.review_title: Formerly a 5 star game. Not anymore.
reviews.review_description:
A review from a former comp player, and battle pass graphic designer for two seasons.

This game is a shadow of its former self. Extremely overpowered metas, pay to win skins, and an inability to listen to the community has torn this game to shreds. Balancing has not been even touched upon slightly in any meaningful way for years now. This leaves players with even just a little bit of experience with the ability to curbstomp new players into the ground before they even see it coming. This scares off new potential community members. 

There is no quality control for the community made maps and skins coming into the game. Blatantly pay to win skins are in the shop, along with a new community made content being a complete annihilation of the game’s art style. I have worked directly with the devs as a graphic designer on two of the battle passes, making a large portion of both, and was neither credited, and was only compensated for my hours of work with 5 dollars of in game currency. 

The competitive players are toxic jerks who spit on anyone who is good at the game and doesn’t follow their strict meta. They are crybabies and will bully people in any way that they can. 

I am currently making a long YouTube video going over everything that needs fixed and changed, and I will link it here till it is done.

Do better Gun Raiders. Bring back the outstanding game we used to love. 

Giving a high rating because I don’t want this game review bombed too hard. I want it to live, and I have hope for the future. 


Original review from 2022: I think I reviewed this game within my first few days of playing it, but that was… shoot… 6 months ago. I want to start this book report off by saying that Gun Raiders is an outstanding game. It takes movement to the next level with its amazing mechanics, and the armory is just amazing. There are tons of available levels, and various gamemodes of all types. The in game currency isn’t overpriced, and the battle pass provides plenty of free rewards for people who don’t want to spend money. Everyone has the same guns, so nobody has the advantage of more powerful weapons and the deva are always working hard to listen to the community and keep everything balenced. The only major problem with the game isn’t an issue with the game itself, it’s the community. The competitive tournament crowd is pretty chill in general, but there’s an army of 2-9 year olds in the game, and there’s always those adults that think they’re king of fork mountain, and talk constant trash they can’t back up. But this is one of the best games ever made, and it gets a full five stars. The community gets a 3 1/2 stars. (There is a mute all players option in the settings to solve this.) -enkarza 2022

reviews.review_title: Still a great game, worth a try and free
reviews.review_description:
Hubs are great for new players to learn the game and have fun. There are mini games that you can activate that gives an interesting change of play for a short time in the hubs. Overshield balances for experience here so players can learn and have fun. 

Arena and BR are better for more experienced players, especially arena. For Arena, there's a variety of team games (my fav) and free for all. Be prepared for players to try hard in arena though. 

Avoid mobile players everywhere as a few are just ridiculous (move hubs). 

Tons of skins are available that the community have made and some maps too. 

The community is mostly welcoming with some longer term players too competitive in arena and BR.

Devs have listened to the community overall but difficult to please all, unfortunately some of the OG players make themselves heard more than others and changes have reflected that, when they don't play the game anymore or are biased for their own gameplay. Also it takes time for changes to happen and many are impatient. 

The game has definitely changed over the years with more weapons and maps. The Devs update the game with more stuff at least 4 times a year. There are community testers of new items/maps too. 

It would be good to have some new features, such as a solo game mode with a basic storyline for GR. This would benefit new players to help them learn and the most popular shooting games have a storyline. 

More features for players to achieve would also help too. Personal weapon stats and achievements.

reviews.review_title: NOT the best game ever anymore 😒
reviews.review_description:
Used to think this game was great, but it’s gone downhill in so many ways… read the other negative complaint, rants and negative reviews to see what I mean… my initial review below is legit but the mobile and pc game hackers, and the ever expanding list of maps and stupid weapons spread the dwindling player base and now hardly anyone is playing it… depending what time you try to ply, there are either no games, a few hubs with noobs or a half full br bus with only the remaining sweats and mobile hackers that just use you as target practice… not what I consider fun 😡 so, I switched to playing Population 1… not the best GME IN THE VR WORLD, BUT LOTS OF FUN AND WAY. ORE PLAYERS 
  I've been a gamer from Space Invaders through to PS1 and PS3 then got a job, had kids, no more games... until Quest and Gun Raiders became a thing... mind blown... total addict... any free moment and the headset is on... watching and learning from the top players and youtubers.
Love the maps and constant new weapons and updates. Learning curve is great... from a fumbling slow newbie always dying mid weapon swap or reload, to gliding through the Temple blasting the young guns and being cursed by my victims as I fly backward out a window SMG blazing annihilating anyone not on my team. The community is generally excellent, good vibe, friendly, welcoming and positive with seasoned players helping new players how to play and improve their gameplay. 
Cannot recommend this game enough!... all hail the developers, please don't stop doing what you do.

Top sniper player here (used to be), and I just wanted to say that im disappointed of how this game turned out over the seasons the devs have provided us. In my opinion, the older seasons were WAY better than the ones now (seasons between 1 and 8 were the best).
If u play the game from a long time, u start to notice u keep seeing the same people over and over again, which it ends up being really boring. The cause of this is because there is 2 or 3 max pages of games (each page containing 12 games in total )
I was a 1v1s player only until the start of this season, I realized it’s not worth to get good at this game because there is really no purpose, it just adds pressure. 
I can relate to this when I was really good with sniper and a whole bunch of people start to ask me to 1v1 them, at first, I did ask for a 1v1 but that was like 3 seasons ago, literally last season there were players begging me to 1v1 them and it was just really annoying ngl.
I feel like I did a great decision by just quitting 1v1s this season, like I went from yelling to being chill.
The first 8 season were my favs, after that, I don’t think nobody liked it, especially when they added the scythe, flame cannons, lightning chaser (gay chaser), crystal, and the hammer.
 I did meet a lot of friends that I could trust over time, and since I quit 1v1, I don’t really have beef with nobody which is quite nice, maybe a few randoms new to the game now in then.
I think there is no solution to this game at all, especially now that I know that the next season Is going to have even more broken weapons and I guess another ai sort related map.
All in all, just disappointed
There's a buncha tryhards so if your new don't play.

Gun Raiders was an immensely popular VR shooter, but when you actually try and put time into it, you really learn how bad it is and how much sense it makes for a game so poorly made to be free to play. The cosmetics are overpriced, the balancing sucks, the gunplay feels illogical and hit or miss, the playerbase is annoying and toxic and the game is butt-ugly. Easy to miss in 2025 and for good reason since it's anything but a good game.

I never realized how much of an absolute clown fest of an idea this was until recently, because what the hell were y'all thinking? I'd have less of an issue with this if PC players had access to this game because at that point what's the fun in playing VR when you're at that big of a disadvantage surrounded by people that have as steady aim as MACHINES when you have to use actual skill to even line up a shot on a stationary target. Absolute buffoonery and clown business what were yall thinking???
